title: Hivekeep Plugin Manifest
description: Schema for plugin.json. Hivekeep loads, validates, and registers plugins based on this manifest. Plugin authors get autocomplete and inline validation in any editor that supports JSON Schema (VSCode, JetBrains, …) by referencing this $id at the top of their plugin.json.

$schema.description: Pointer to this JSON Schema. Editors use it for autocomplete + validation.

name.description: Unique plugin identifier. Lowercase, digits, dashes. Hivekeep uses it as the directory name in plugins/ and as the namespace prefix for the plugin's tools, providers, and vault keys.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI (e.g. "Mistral AI", "Notion Sync"). When omitted, Hivekeep falls back to the technical `name`.

description.description: Short user-facing description surfaced in the Plugins UI.

main.description: Entry file (relative to the plugin directory). Hivekeep dynamically imports it at activation time. Default convention: "index.ts".

homepage.description: URL surfaced in the Plugins UI.

hivekeep.description: Semver range of Hivekeep host versions this plugin is compatible with (e.g. ">=0.40.0"). Hivekeep blocks activation when the host version is outside the range and surfaces the mismatch in the UI.

icon.description: Display icon. Either an emoji ("🔔") or a path to a file inside the plugin directory.

iconUrl.description: Path to a logo image inside the plugin directory (e.g. "logo.svg"). Hivekeep serves it via GET /api/plugins/<name>/logo and surfaces it in the Plugins UI and the npm Browse tab.

items.description: A permission grant. Hivekeep enforces http:* permissions at runtime through ctx.http.fetch: calls to undeclared hosts throw before going out. Other grants are informational today and will be enforced in future releases.

permissions.description: Permissions the plugin requires. Each entry is either an http permission ("http:api.example.com", or "http:*" for any host) or one of the named capabilities ("storage", "cards", "vault", "cron", "agents").

dependencies.description: Other plugins this one depends on, keyed by plugin name with a semver range value. Hivekeep resolves the dependency graph and refuses to activate a plugin whose dependencies are missing or out of range.

config.description: Plugin-level configuration schema. The Plugins UI renders a settings form from this; users edit, Hivekeep persists, then hands the resolved values to your plugin via ctx.config.

channels.description: Per-channel-adapter metadata declared at manifest level. The most common use is declaring the channel's configSchema here so it's discoverable without running the plugin's entry point.

tags.description: Free-form tags surfaced in the Plugins UI.

PluginConfigField.description: A single field in the plugin's config form. Discriminated by `type`.

secret.description: Marks the field's value as sensitive: Hivekeep encrypts it at rest and masks it in logs.

PluginChannelEntry.description: Optional metadata for a channel adapter the plugin contributes.
